"'''Death Star Rising'''" was a [[Music/Legends|song]] by the pro-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Minga (music)|minga]] [[Band/Legends|musical group]] [[The Emperor's New Clothes]]. In a display of bad taste, the song was released within [[Galactic Standard Calendar|days]] after the Empire's [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]<ref name="slugthrowers" /> by the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> It was featured on the album ''[[Artistic Integrity]]'', which was the band's latest release<ref name="GG9" /> at some point in [[Time/Legends|time]] between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'' is set in the [[Time/Legends|time period]] between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which are dated to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], respectively, by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which also establishes the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] as the final event before the beginning of [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].</ref>

Like all of The [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] New [[Clothing/Legends|Clothes']] music, "Death Star Rising" was approved by the [[Imperial Board of Culture]].<ref name="GG9" /> The content and nature of the song led some observers to believe a closer relationship between The Emperor's New Clothes and the Galactic Empire.<ref name="slugthrowers" />

==Behind the scenes==
"Death Star Rising" was one of the songs by The Emperor's New Clothes that were listed in the ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', a [[1993]] supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. ''Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim'' was written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]].<ref name="GG9" />
